changes.value:
## What

After Cards 1-3, inspector stack persistence happens as a side-effect of `dispatch_command` (Card 1) and the frontend no longer calls `set_inspector_stack` directly (Card 2). The separate `set_inspector_stack` Tauri command is now dead code.

### Files
- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` — remove `set_inspector_stack` function
- `kanban-app/src/main.rs` — remove from `.invoke_handler(tauri::generate_handler![...])`

### Approach
1. Remove the `set_inspector_stack` function from `commands.rs`
2. Remove it from the Tauri invoke handler registration in `main.rs`
3. Verify no frontend code still references it (should be clean after Card 2)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `set_inspector_stack` Tauri command no longer exists
- [ ] No frontend code references `set_inspector_stack`
- [ ] `cargo nextest run` passes
- [ ] `npx vitest run` passes

## Tests
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` — compile success confirms no dangling references
- [ ] `npx vitest run` — no test references the removed command

## Subtasks
- [ ] Remove `set_inspector_stack` from `commands.rs`
- [ ] Remove from Tauri handler registration in `main.rs`
- [ ] Grep for any remaining references

## depends_on
- Card 2: Frontend route inspect/close through dispatch_command"
